_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test coverage, performance implications, documentation completeness, and adherence to team coding standards.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-A-003 (AB, cosine: 0.464), AB-T-009 (AB, cosine: 0.437).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.1/10 (raw 0.511). Shared vocabulary: [conduct, reviews]. DCWF-only terms [equity, profile] vs WIDAI-only terms [adherence, application, changes, code, coding, completeness] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.849 (P=0.875, R=0.825). P exceeds R by 0.050 — the DCWF element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (96.2%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Conduct profile/equity reviews." and "Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.465 ≈ STS 0.511 (gap 0.046). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [conduct, reviews] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.7%. Shared tokens: [conduct, reviews]. DCWF-only: [equity, profile]. WIDAI-only: [adherence, application, changes, code, coding, completeness].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 96.2% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
